text:
Once upon a time in a cozy little village called Meadowgrove, there lived a wise old owl named Luna. Luna had feathers of softest silver and always knew everything about birds. She had two particular favorite toys: a broken rocking horse and a miniature clock with a big face that never stopped ticking.

One morning, Luna found an old diary in a forgotten library shelf. As she turned the tiny wheels with her beak, she discovered that it was written by a girl named Amelia. Amelia loved owls. She often drew pictures of Luna in her diary.

After a rainy week, Amelia wrote back, saying how much she misspoke owl-eyed. She drew a beautiful picture of Luna perched on a branch over her bed.

Luna loved that little book. Years went by, and Amelia grew into a wise lady. When she retired, she moved to a cottage on a quiet lake.

One day, hearing fluttering noise, Amelia opened her window and found a gorgeous snowy owl perched
